NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO NEAR WILTON, NY. SEVERAL SOFTWOOD TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED (SOME ONTO HOUSES), AND STREET SIGNS WERE BLOWN DOWN AND DISPLACED. ALSO, PART OF THE SHEETING OF A WAREHOUSE ROOF WAS PEELED OFF AND LANDED ON A VAN, AND AN EMPTY TRAILER WAS BLOWN ON TOP OF A VAN. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.8 MILES.

MULTIPLE REPORTS OF TORNADO SIGHTNINGS REPORTED TO 911 CENTER.
MULTIPLE REPORTS OF TORNADO SIGHTNINGS REPORTED TO 911 CENTER. NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM ON 8/3/2020 CONFIRMED EF1 TORNADO.

THE NWS ALBANY RECEIVED PHOTO AND EYEWITNESS ACCOUNTS TODAY CONSISTENT WITH A TORNADO. THE NWS DID CONDUCT A SURVEY OF THIS AREA ON AUGUST 3, 2020, BUT COULD NOT LOCATE ANY DAMAGE. NOW WITH THIS INFORMATION, THE NWS CONFIRMS AN EF0 TORNADO, APPROXIMATELY 0.5 MILE IN LENGTH AND ESTIMATED 100 YARDS IN WIDTH. THIS TORNADO OCCURRED WITH THE SAME SUPERCELL THAT PRODUCED TORNADOES IN SHARON, CT, FALLS VILLAGE, CT, SANDISFIELD, MA, AND HAMPDEN, MA. NWS THANKS OUR PARTNERS FOR BRINGING THIS TO OUR ATTE
TORNADO VERIFIED BY NWS BOSTON STORM SURVEY. TORNADO CONTINUED INTO HAMPDEN COUNTY,MA. RATED EF0. TORNADO WAS IN BERKSHIRE COUNTY FOR APPROXIMATELY 1/4 MILE.

DAMAGE PHOTOS SUPPLIED BY THE PUBLIC SUGGEST THAT A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO OCCURRED ON QUANOPAUG TRAIL IN WOODBURY. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED IN VARIOUS DIRECTIONS IN A WOODED AREA. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 85 MILES PER HOUR WITH A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS.

AN EF0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 85 MILES PER HOUR OCCURRED IN THE TOWN OF HARTMAN, NY, IN WARREN COUNTY. THERE WAS VIDEO EVIDENCE AND AN EYEWITNESS REPORT OF A FUNNEL KICKING UP WATER ON THE HUDSON RIVER JUST SOUTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF BEACH DRIVE AND PLEASANT VIEW ROAD. THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST AND CROSSED EAST RIVER DRIVE BEFORE ENDING. ABOUT A DOZEN TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. ONE HOMEOWNER REPORTED AN OUTDOOR CANOPY DESTROYED AND LAWN FURNITURE SCATTERED.

THE STORM SURVEY TEAM OBSERVED A NEARLY DESTROYED BARN WITH METAL SIDING STREWN ABOUT THE PROPERTY. A TREE WAS ALSO TOPPLED. THIS WAS THE ONLY DAMAGE. THE TORNADO WAS VERY SHORT-LIVED. IT SPUN UP ON THE QLCS LINE MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.

A MICROBURST ALONG CROLL ROAD, EAST OF TOMHANNOCK RESERVOIR IN PITTSTOWN,PRODUCED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE ON OCT 7, 2020. THE MICROBURST MOVED IN A GENERAL WEST SOUTHWEST TO EAST NORTHEAST DIRECTION. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS TO TREES. DAMAGE INCLUDED NUMEROUS SOFTWOOD TREES SHEARED OFF, A FEW HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREES UPROOTED AND SOME MINOR DIRECT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO HOMES INCLUDING SHINGLES BUCKLED UP FROM ONE HOME AND FLASHING PEELED AWAY FROM ANOTHER HOME.
